July l, 2008
David Morton
County of San Diego/Registrar of Voters
Financial Disclosure Desk
5201 Ruffin Road, Suite I
San Diego, CA 92123
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-08-108
Dear Mr. Morton
This letter is in response to your request regarding the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the ..4"1,r;.1 -
QUESTTON
May the committees involved in an Augu st 26,2008. mail ballot election combine
the semi-annual statement with the first pre-election statement due on July 17, 2oog?
CONCLUSION
In connection with the August 26,20o8,mail ballot election, committees may use
a filing schedule that combines tha semi-annual campaign statement with the first pre-
election statement. The combined statement will belue on July 17, 200g, covering the
period January l, 2008, through July 12, 200g.
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code sections g 1000 through g l0l4. All
statutory ref'erences are to the Government code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulatirns of the Fair
Political Practices commission are c,ntained in Sections r 8 r r0 ilr;;;t 
t ssqz of ritle 2 of the Califbrnia
Code of Regttlations. All regulatory ref'erences are ro Tirle 2, Divisioi 6 of the califbrnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-08-108
Page No. 2
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are
required to file two pre-election campaign statements disclosing contributions received
and expenditures made in connection wilh that election. These'pre-.tr"tion 
"umpaign.
statements are general! r1lea 40 days before the election and agiin 12 days uero.e ttre
election' (Section 84200.8.) semi-annual statements are filed January 3l and July 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)
There is a mechanism in the Act that permits the combining of statements when
an overlapping reporting period occurs or the filing of the semi-annual statement would
be an additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-"election statements. (Section g4205.)
In this case, if the semi-annual and first pre-elettion statements are not combined, the
semi-annual statement, covering the period through lu"" :0, zoog,will be filed on
July 31, 2008, which is after the first pre-election statement is due. Therefore,
committees involved in the August 26,20o8,mail ballot election may combine the two
filings' A campaign statement iiting schedule that outlines the due dates for filing
statements in connection with the erection is enclosed for your convenience.

ANALYSIS
